title: Simple Interest Calculator

meta_description: Calculate simple interest on any principal over any time period quickly and accurately.

hero.title: Simple Interest Calculator
hero.subtitle: A straightforward tool to determine the exact interest accumulated on an initial principal without compounding.

formula_explanation: Simple interest is calculated uniquely on the original principal. The interest does not compound or grow over time.
example: If you invest $1,000 at a 5% simple annual rate for 2 years, you'll earn exactly $100 in interest ($50 each year).

faq.question: What's the difference between simple and compound interest?
faq.answer: Simple interest is calculated only on the initial principal. Compound interest is calculated on both the principal and the accumulated interest.

faq.question: Where is simple interest actually used?
faq.answer: It is often used in auto loans, short-term personal loans, and penalties for late payments where interest does not stack on top of interest.

faq.question: Why do banks prefer compound interest?
faq.answer: Compound interest charges interest on previously accrued interest, allowing banks to maximize returns over the lifespan of a debt or investment.

related_tools.title: Compound Interest Calculator
